chapter_title.ar: بَابُ لَوْ لَا أَنَّ الْأَئِمَّةَ ( عليهم السلام ) يَزْدَادُونَ لَنَفِدَ مَا عِنْدَهُمْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 43 – If the Imams<sup>asws</sup> were not to be increased, it would deplete what is with them<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/107)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/107)
text: لَيْسَ يَخْرُجُ شَيْ‏ءٌ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ حَتَّى يَبْدَأَ بِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) ثُمَّ بِأَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) ثُمَّ بِوَاحِدٍ بَعْدَ وَاحِدٍ لِكَيْلَا يَكُونَ آخِرُنَا أَعْلَمَ مِنْ أَوَّلِنَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus Bin Abdul Rahman, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘There isn’t anything coming out from the Presence of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic until it is begun with Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, then with Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>, then by one after another, lest our<sup>asws</sup> later ones should be more knowledgeable than our former ones’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus ibn ‘Abd al-Rahman from some of his people from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said the following. “Nothing comes from Allah, the Most Holy, the Most High, except that first it begins with the Messenger of Allah then Amir al-Mu’minin Ali (a.s.) then the Imams one after the other so that the last us would not be more knowledgeable than the first of us.”
